perturbation.original_text: 1. Definitions. Any capitalized term used but not defined in this Amendment will have the meaning set forth for that term in the Non-Competition Agreement or the Master Framework Agreement, dated September 30, 2016, by and between the Parties (the "Master Framework Agreement").
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Definitions. Any capitalized term used but not defined in this Amendment will have the meaning determined by the Parties based on common usage.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition clause to rely on 'common usage' instead of the 'Non-Competition Agreement or the Master Framework Agreement', any defined terms within those agreements are now ambiguous. This creates uncertainty and contradicts the intent of referring back to those agreements for clarity, therefore it introduces an in-text contradiction. This is especially relevant as the agreement itself references these external agreements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Master Framework Agreement. This Amendment is governed by the Master Framework Agreement, including, without limitation, the provisions of Sections 4 (Confidentiality) and 6 (Miscellaneous) of the Master Framework Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the explicit reference to the Master Framework Agreement for defining terms, instead relying on 'common usage'. The contradicted text states that the Amendment is governed by the Master Framework Agreement, including specific sections. This creates a direct contradiction because the changed text establishes a different, incompatible method for interpreting the agreement's terms, undermining the governing role of the Master Framework Agreement as stated in the contradicted text. This is a strong in-text contradiction because it introduces ambiguity where the contradicted clause expects certainty of the meaning derived from the Master Framework Agreement. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' type.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Non-Solicitation. Section 4 of the Non-Competition Agreement is hereby deleted in its entirety and replaced with the following: "Term. This Agreement will become effective on the Effective Date, and will continue until the expiration of the "Sales Term" as that term is defined in the Sales Dealer Agreement dated as of August 16, 2017 between Vivint and Vivint Solar Developer, LLC (the "Term")."
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Non-Solicitation. Section 4 of the Non-Competition Agreement is hereby deleted in its entirety and replaced with the following: "Term. This Agreement will become effective on the Effective Date, and will continue for a period of three years (the "Term")."
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the reference to the 'Sales Term' defined in the Sales Dealer Agreement and replacing it with a fixed three-year term, the contract introduces a contradiction. Other sections might rely on the definition of 'Sales Term' for determining the agreement's duration or triggering certain clauses. This creates an in-text contradiction because the initial agreement was meant to depend on Sales Term, and now is stating fixed terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Continuation. This Amendment will apply and be effective only with respect to the provisions of the Non- Competition Agreement specifically referred to herein. Except as otherwise set forth in this Amendment, the Non-Competition Agreement will continue in full force and effect in accordance with its terms.
perturbation.justification: The original text links the term of the agreement to the 'Sales Term' defined in another agreement. The changed text replaces this with a fixed three-year term. The contradicted text states that the Non-Competition Agreement will continue in full force and effect in accordance with its terms, which originally included the 'Sales Term'. By removing the link to the 'Sales Term' and establishing a fixed term, the changed text contradicts the contradicted text, which anticipates the Non-Competition Agreement to continue as per its original terms, creating a direct conflict as the duration is no longer tied to the Sales Term but to a fixed term. This is classified as an Omission - In Text Contradiction according to the provided definitions.
